mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months or the t-statistic for the return difference falls below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods (Rank IC < 0.01) or the signal reverses direction in expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL index reading above 220.0 triggers immediate equity sell-offs as investors anticipate restrictive monetary policy shifts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation months or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP levels falling below 13000.0 billion chained dollars during a late-cycle expansion confirm economic deceleration, triggering risk premium increases and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of low-GDP months or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases or the rolling 1-year correlation falls below 0.05.
